2606.09396.title: PriFT: Prior-Support Guided Supervised Fine-Tuning
2606.09396.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is an efficient approach for downstream task adaptation and often serves as the initialization stage for reinforcement learning (RL), but it can show weaker generalization than RL. A key limitation is its off-policy objective: SFT fits fixed demonstrations token by token, including targets poorly aligned with the model's pretrained distribution, which can lead to overfitting. A recent line of work addresses this issue by assigning larger training weights to tokens better aligned with the current model's predictive distribution, with the intuition that fitting these tokens are less distortive to the model's pretrained knowledge and representations. However, computing the token weights from the model that is currently fine-tuned entangles token weights with the optimization trajectory, inducing a self-reinforcing dynamics as the distribution rapidly departs from the pretrained model. To address this, we propose PriFT (Prior-support guided Fine-Tuning), which derives token weights from a frozen pretrained reference to obtain a stable reweighting signal unaffected by fine-tuning. This signal estimates prior support: the extent to which each target token is supported by the pretrained distribution. Across multiple existing token-reweighting rules, replacing the reweighting signal from the online model to pretrained model consistently improves performance. We introduce two instantiations: PriFT-prob uses pretrained token probability, while PriFT-mass selects tokens by cumulative probability mass under the pretrained distribution. Extensive experiments on mathematical reasoning, code generation, and medical question answering show that PriFT achieves state-of-the-art results among SFT baselines and provides a better initialization for subsequent RL training.
2606.09396.paper_summary_zh: 監督微調（SFT）是一種有效的下游任務適應方法，通常作為強化學習（RL）的初始化階段，但其泛化能力可能不如RL。其一個主要限制是其離線策略目標：SFT逐個令牌擬合固定的示範，包括與模型的預訓練分佈不良對齊的目標，這可能導致過擬合。最近的一系列研究通過為與當前模型的預測分佈更好對齊的令牌分配更大的訓練權重來解決這個問題，其直覺是擬合這些令牌對模型的預訓練知識和表示的扭曲較小。然而，從當前微調的模型計算令牌權重會使令牌權重與優化軌跡交織在一起，隨著分佈迅速偏離預訓練模型，產生自我強化的動態。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了PriFT（先驗支持引導微調），它從一個凍結的預訓練參考中推導令牌權重，以獲得不受微調影響的穩定重加權信號。這個信號估計先驗支持：每個目標令牌在多大程度上受到預訓練分佈的支持。在多種現有的令牌重加權規則中，將重加權信號從在線模型替換為預訓練模型始終能改善性能。我們介紹了兩種實例：PriFT-prob使用預訓練令牌概率，而PriFT-mass則根據預訓練分佈下的累積概率質量選擇令牌。在數學推理、代碼生成和醫療問答等領域的廣泛實驗顯示，PriFT在SFT基準中達到了最先進的結果，並為後續的RL訓練提供了更好的初始化。
2606.09396.author: Ke Wang et.al.
2606.09396.authors: Ke Wang, Shuangqi Li, Mathieu Salzmann, Pascal Frossard
